research.interface: Meraki Dashboard API v1 REST (organizations, networks, device availabilities)

official_sources.title: Getting Started - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: primary base URL https://api.meraki.com/api/v1 and the regional base URIs api.meraki.ca, api.meraki.cn, api.meraki.in, api.gov-meraki.com (all with /api/v1); bearer authorization header format Authorization: Bearer <Meraki_API_Key>

official_sources.title: Rate Limit - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: 10 requests per second per organization with a burst of 30 in 2 seconds; HTTP 429 on exceeding the limit with a Retry-After header giving seconds to wait and body {"errors": ["API rate limit exceeded for organization"]}

official_sources.title: Pagination - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: perPage/startingAfter/endingBefore query parameters; RFC 5988 Link response header carrying up to first, prev, next, last relations whose URLs should be used directly rather than rebuilt

official_sources.title: Get Organizations - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /organizations with perPage 3-9000; response objects carry id, name, url, api.enabled, licensing.model, cloud.region.name

official_sources.title: Get Organization Networks - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /organizations/{organizationId}/networks with perPage 3-100000; response objects carry id, organizationId, name, productTypes, timeZone, tags

official_sources.title: Get Organization Devices Availabilities - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /organizations/{organizationId}/devices/availabilities (the current replacement for the deprecated devices/statuses operation); perPage 3-1000 default 1000; productTypes/statuses/networkIds/serials/tags array filters; response objects {mac, name, network.id, productType, serial, status, tags} with status enum online, alerting, offline, dormant

research.language_rationale: This inventory job lives in a Python ops toolchain. The Dashboard API is documented as plain REST with bearer auth, Link-header pagination, and a JSON errors envelope, and Meraki's official Python library is a thin wrapper over exactly these endpoints; implementing against the documented REST contract with the stdlib keeps the acceptance tests hermetic and dependency-free while exercising the same wire semantics the official SDK relies on.

verified_facts: The Dashboard API v1 global base URL is https://api.meraki.com/api/v1; regional dashboards use api.meraki.ca (Canada), api.meraki.cn (China), api.meraki.in (India) and api.gov-meraki.com (US FedRAMP), all with the /api/v1 base path.
Every v1 request authenticates with the standard Authorization header whose value is 'Bearer ' followed by the API key; v0's X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key header is obsolete.
The per-organization rate limit is 10 requests per second (burst 30 in 2 seconds); exceeding it returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header in seconds and an errors-array body.
Paginated operations take perPage/startingAfter/endingBefore and answer with an RFC 5988 Link header of up to first/prev/next/last relations whose URLs are meant to be followed as given.

GET /organizations/{organizationId}/devices/statuses is deprecated; the current device status listing is GET /organizations/{organizationId}/devices/availabilities returning {mac, name, network.id, productType, serial, status, tags}.
Device availability status is one of online, alerting, offline, dormant, and results can be filtered with repeated productTypes[] and statuses[] array query parameters.

GET /organizations returns id, name, url, api.enabled, licensing.model and cloud.region.name for each organization; GET /organizations/{organizationId}/networks returns id, name and productTypes per network.
